vram_feasibility.note: PR #166's bigger-GPU routing was unnecessary for the LoRA path; it fits 8 GB with ~2x headroom even at batch=16.

data_wiring_fix.rename_map: libero_10 names its 2nd camera wrist_image (not image2); map image->camera1, wrist_image->camera2
data_wiring_fix.use_imagenet_stats: set false to match the published smolvla_libero train_config.json AND to avoid a KeyError: libero_10's cached stats.json predates the image-stat keys; SmolVLA uses VISUAL:IDENTITY so image stats are never consumed
data_wiring_fix.verified: single training step runs clean; loss converges (1e-4/3000: 0.166->0.058; 1e-5/1000: ~0.078)

diagnosis.eval_path_control: published zero-shot smolvla_libero through the IDENTICAL eval path scores 8/20 on seed0 (pooled 0.252 across 5 seeds) -> the eval path + merge are correct; the 0% is a genuine fine-tune regression, not an artifact.
diagnosis.action_stats: fine-tuned actions are sane in magnitude (absmax ~0.86, comparable to zero-shot ~1.0) but shifted, notably the gripper dim (-0.99 zero-shot -> +0.57 fine-tuned) -> closed-loop grasping fails while BC loss stays low.
diagnosis.lr_ruled_out: both an aggressive (1e-4) and a gentle (1e-5) LR collapse, so it is structural, not a hyperparameter-tuning miss.
diagnosis.image_orientation_ruled_out: eval flips env images 180 to match the dataset orientation (LiberoProcessorStep / _libero_obs_to_batch), identically for zero-shot and fine-tuned.
diagnosis.remaining_hypothesis: a subtle train-vs-closed-loop observation/action distribution gap that the already-marginal smolvla_libero checkpoint tolerates zero-shot but a light LoRA fine-tune destabilizes (low open-loop BC loss, 0 closed-loop success). Not resolvable within the local time box; would need the upstream author's exact training-eval matched pipeline / a longer LR-warmup + more steps sweep on a bigger GPU.
diagnosis.merge_ruled_out: the LoRA adapter loaded directly via PeftModel (NO merge_and_unload) also scores 0/15 on seed0 -> the merge is correct; the collapse is intrinsic to the fine-tune.

lift.read: This is a fine-tuning COLLAPSE on the weak cell, not a lift and not an ordering inversion. A clean negative: the VRAM + data-wiring blockers PR #166 cited are both solved locally, but a working fine-tune needs more than the rename-map fix.

contract.note: full 5x50 re-measure aborted: a 0% policy makes every episode run the full 520-step cap (~20 min/seed, ~100 min total) which exceeds the time box; seed-0 partials (0/50 at 1e-4, 0/20 and 0/15 at the gentler recipes) are conclusive.

time_box.note: training is well within box; the open-loop fine-tune is cheap, but a *working* closed-loop fine-tune was not achieved locally.
